goal: Wire the `check_quality` and `type_check` MCP tools into the daemon's `handle_tools_call` dispatch path: F10 fans out to the existing `execute_g7`+`execute_g8` backbones in parallel and projects results into a `{issues[], untested_functions[]}` JSON-RPC response; F15 filters the existing LSP diagnostics bridge output to type errors only and projects to a `{errors[]}` response covering Python, TypeScript, and Rust.
plan_summary: Two-feature coordinated bundle around master-plan §3.2 row 14 (`check_quality`) + row 18 (`type_check`) and §18 Phase 3 Week 11 item 6 (Wire G7/G8 into `check_quality`). F10 adds a NEW handler `McpServer::handle_check_quality(&self, id: &Value, params: &Value, g7_sources: &[Box<dyn G7Source + Send + Sync>], g8_sources: &[Box<dyn G8Source + Send + Sync>], master_deadline: Duration) -> Value` placed adjacent to `handle_find_definition` in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs`, calling `execute_g7` + `execute_g8` IN PARALLEL via `tokio::try_join!` (or `tokio::join!` since both already tolerate per-source failures internally), running `merge_g7_by_severity` + `merge_g8_test_discoveries` on the outputs, and projecting to the canonical `CheckQualityResponse { issues: Vec<QualityIssueWire>, untested_functions: Vec<UntestedFunctionWire>, meta: { master_timed_out: bool, wall_elapsed_ms: u64 } }` shape with serde-derived JSON. F15 adds a NEW handler `McpServer::handle_type_check(&self, id: &Value, params: &Value, diagnostics_client: &DiagnosticsClient) -> Value` placed adjacent to F10's handler, calling `DiagnosticsClient::diagnostics(file_path, language)` for each input file, filtering each returned `Diagnostic` to keep only `severity == DiagnosticSeverity::Error` AND a category matching the type-error allow-list (`source` ∈ {`rust-analyzer`, `pyright`, `tsserver`, `typescript`} OR `code` matching one of the language-specific type-error code prefixes per master-plan §3.2 row 18). Both handlers wire into the `handle_tools_call` `match name` chain (positional after `find_definition` / `get_conventions` / `understand_code` / `find_references` / `search_code` / `find_similar` per the existing dispatch order at server.rs:784-790). Frozen tests `test_check_quality_tool` (F10) + `test_type_check_tool` (F15) at MODULE ROOT of `server.rs` per DEC-0007 substring-selector resolution + WO-0085 G7 / WO-0089 G8 module-root precedent. Each test seeds the dispatch path with `TestG7Source` / `TestG8Source` impls (carried forward from WO-0085 / WO-0089) for F10 OR a `ScriptedFakeSerenaClient` (carried forward from WO-0048 / WO-0085) for F15 and asserts the JSON-RPC response shape end-to-end. Mutation contract: M1 (F10 — drop G7 from the parallel join — SA fails: `issues` empty when TestG7Source emits them); M2 (F10 — drop G8 from the parallel join — SA fails: `untested_functions` empty when TestG8Source emits them); M3 (F15 — drop the severity+source filter — SA fails: low-severity Warning leaks into the `errors` array). All three reversible via `git checkout --` + md5sum verify per WO-0085 / WO-0086 verifier protocol.
scope_in: 1) Add `pub async fn handle_check_quality(&self, id: &Value, params: &Value) -> Value` to `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs`, placed adjacent to `handle_find_definition` (line ~891). The handler MUST: (a) parse `target` (string, required) + `reason` (string, required per CEQP) + optional `current_task` / `files_in_context` / `token_budget` from `params.arguments`; (b) construct a `g7::G7Query { ... }` and `g8::G8Query { changed_files: ... }` from the `target`; (c) fan out `execute_g7` + `execute_g8` IN PARALLEL via `tokio::join!` (NOT sequential — see lessons from WO-0067/0068/0070 on mutation-contract surface); (d) call `merge_g7_by_severity(&g7_outcome)` + `merge_g8_test_discoveries(&g8_outcome)`; (e) project to the canonical wire shape `{ issues: [{ severity, category, file, line, fix_suggestion, source_tools }], untested_functions: [{ test_path, source_path, methods_found_by, max_confidence }], meta: { master_timed_out, wall_elapsed_ms } }`; (f) return a JSON-RPC `Value` with `result.content[0].text = serde_json::to_string(...)`. The handler MUST NOT panic on empty G7/G8 source lists — return an empty `issues[]` / `untested_functions[]` array.

2) Add `pub async fn handle_type_check(&self, id: &Value, params: &Value) -> Value` to `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs`, placed adjacent to F10's handler. The handler MUST: (a) parse `files: Vec<String>` (required, list of file paths) + `reason` (string, required per CEQP) + optional CEQP fields from `params.arguments`; (b) for each file, call `DiagnosticsClient::diagnostics(&PathBuf::from(file), Language::from_path(file))` and collect the returned `Vec<Diagnostic>`; (c) filter each `Diagnostic` to keep ONLY ones where `severity == DiagnosticSeverity::Error` AND (`source` ∈ {`rust-analyzer`, `pyright`, `tsserver`, `typescript`, `mypy`} OR `code` matches one of the language-specific type-error code prefixes — `E` codes for Rust + `Pyright`/`reportXxx` for Python + `TS2xxx` / `TS7xxx` for TypeScript per master-plan §3.2 row 18 spec language: `"Type checking diagnostics via LSP diagnostics bridge"`); (d) project to wire shape `{ errors: [{ file, line, message, language, severity }], meta: { files_checked: usize, files_skipped: usize } }`; (e) return JSON-RPC `Value`. Files lacking an LSP-supported language MUST be in the `files_skipped` count, not an error.

3) Wire BOTH handlers into the `handle_tools_call` `if/else if name == "..."` dispatch chain in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs:778+`. Position: AFTER all G1/G2/G5 tool routes (`find_definition`, `get_conventions`, `understand_code`, `find_references`, `search_code`, `find_similar`) AND BEFORE the generic fall-through. The chain must remain `if/else if` (NO `match` rewrite) — preserves diff size + critic check 5 (Public API) precedent.

4) `McpServer` struct gains TWO new fields under `#[cfg(test)]` carve-out: `pub(crate) g7_sources: Vec<Box<dyn G7Source + Send + Sync>>` + `pub(crate) g8_sources: Vec<Box<dyn G8Source + Send + Sync>>` + `pub(crate) diagnostics_client: Option<Arc<DiagnosticsClient>>`. NON-test production paths default to empty Vec / None. The TestG7Source / TestG8Source / ScriptedFakeSerenaClient injection is via builder-style `with_g7_sources(self, sources: Vec<...>)`, `with_g8_sources(...)`, `with_diagnostics_client(...)` methods. Mirror the WO-0083 (G4) / WO-0085 (G7) / WO-0089 (G8) builder pattern verbatim. Production-wiring of REAL `LspDiagnosticsG7Source` / `EslintG7Source` / `RuffG7Source` / `SemgrepG7Source` / `ConventionTestG8Source` / etc into daemon startup is OUT OF SCOPE for this WO (see scope_out #2 — same deferral as WO-0083/0085/0089).

5) DEC-0007 frozen-test-at-module-root: BOTH `test_check_quality_tool` (F10) + `test_type_check_tool` (F15) live as `#[cfg(test)] #[tokio::test] async fn test_check_quality_tool() { ... }` AND `#[cfg(test)] #[tokio::test] async fn test_type_check_tool() { ... }` at MODULE ROOT of `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs` (NOT inside `mod tests {}`). The substring selector `cargo test -p ucil-daemon server::test_check_quality_tool` MUST resolve uniquely from a `cargo clean` baseline; same for `server::test_type_check_tool`. Acceptance test selectors in `feature-list.json` are `cargo_test -p ucil-daemon server::test_check_quality_tool` (F10) + `cargo_test -p ucil-daemon server::test_type_check_tool` (F15) — the test name MUST match those selectors verbatim.

6) `test_check_quality_tool` MUST: (a) construct `TestG7Source` impls returning a deterministic `Vec<G7Issue>` (e.g., 3 issues spanning Critical/High/Medium severities — same vocabulary as WO-0085 SA6 sentinel: `vec![Critical, High, Medium]`); (b) construct `TestG8Source` impls returning a deterministic `Vec<G8TestCandidate>` (e.g., 2 candidates with distinct test_path / source_path / methods); (c) build an `McpServer` via `.with_g7_sources(...).with_g8_sources(...)`; (d) construct a `tools/call` JSON-RPC payload `{name: "check_quality", arguments: {target: "src/foo.rs", reason: "verifier smoke"}}`; (e) call `server.handle_line(&payload).await`; (f) parse the response.result.content[0].text as JSON and assert SA1-SA6: SA1 — `issues[]` length == 3 (per the seeded G7 source); SA2 — `issues[0].severity` == `"critical"` (lowercase per §5.7 vocabulary canary); SA3 — `untested_functions[]` length == 2 (per the seeded G8 source); SA4 — `untested_functions[0].test_path` matches the seeded test; SA5 — `meta.master_timed_out == false`; SA6 — `meta.wall_elapsed_ms < 5000` (parallelism wall-clock canary — sequential awaits would exceed 2 × per-source-deadline). Each `assert!` / `assert_eq!` MUST carry the `(SAn) <semantic name>; left: ...; right: ...` panic-body shape per DEC-0007 + WO-0067 mature pattern.

7) `test_type_check_tool` MUST: (a) construct a `ScriptedFakeSerenaClient` (carried from WO-0048 / WO-0085 — DO NOT re-implement; the carry is now 6 WOs deep) returning a deterministic `Vec<Diagnostic>` per file containing AT LEAST: one Error-severity Diagnostic with `source = "rust-analyzer"` (Rust type error), one Error-severity Diagnostic with `source = "pyright"` (Python type error), one Error-severity Diagnostic with `source = "tsserver"` (TypeScript type error), one Warning-severity Diagnostic (lint warning, MUST be filtered out), one Error-severity Diagnostic with `source = "clippy"` (lint error from Rust — NOT a type error, MUST be filtered out); (b) wrap into `DiagnosticsClient::new(Arc::new(fake))`; (c) build `McpServer` via `.with_diagnostics_client(client)`; (d) construct `tools/call` JSON-RPC payload `{name: "type_check", arguments: {files: ["src/foo.rs", "src/bar.py", "src/baz.ts"], reason: "verifier smoke"}}`; (e) call `server.handle_line(&payload).await`; (f) parse response and assert SA1-SA5: SA1 — `errors[]` length == 3 (Rust + Python + TypeScript type errors only — Warning + clippy filtered out); SA2 — every `errors[].severity` == `"error"`; SA3 — `errors[].language` covers `{rust, python, typescript}`; SA4 — `meta.files_checked == 3`; SA5 — `meta.files_skipped == 0`. Same SA-numbered panic-body shape per DEC-0007.

8) Mutation contract M1 + M2 + M3 (3 mutations across 2 features): M1 (F10 G7-route bypass) — patch `let (g7_outcome, g8_outcome) = tokio::join!(execute_g7(...), execute_g8(...));` → `let g8_outcome = execute_g8(...).await; let g7_outcome = G7Outcome { results: vec![], wall_elapsed_ms: 0, master_timed_out: false };` — flips `test_check_quality_tool` PASS → FAIL with `(SA1) issues[] length; left: 0, right: 3`. M2 (F10 G8-route bypass) — symmetric to M1 but on the G8 side — flips `test_check_quality_tool` PASS → FAIL with `(SA3) untested_functions[] length; left: 0, right: 2`. M3 (F15 type-error filter bypass) — patch the `.filter(|d| d.severity == DiagnosticSeverity::Error && IS_TYPE_ERROR_SOURCE(...))` to `.filter(|_| true)` (no filter) — flips `test_type_check_tool` PASS → FAIL with `(SA1) errors[] length; left: 5, right: 3` (the Warning + clippy non-type-error diagnostics now leak into the response). All three reversible via `git checkout -- crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs` + md5sum verify against `/tmp/wo-0090-server-orig.md5sum` snapshot.

9) `crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs` re-export block: ADD `pub use server::{...};` for any NEW public types `CheckQualityResponse`, `TypeCheckResponse`, `QualityIssueWire`, `UntestedFunctionWire`, `TypeError` (the wire-shape projections). Do NOT add re-exports for the `handle_check_quality` / `handle_type_check` async fns themselves — they are method-form on `McpServer`, not free functions.

10) `#[tracing::instrument(name = "ucil.tool.check_quality")]` on `handle_check_quality` per master-plan §15.2 line 1519. Same on `handle_type_check` with `name = "ucil.tool.type_check"`. NO numeric-cast `fields(...)` per WO-0085 §planner lesson — let tracing auto-capture fn args. Inside the handler body, span-attach the `target` / `files` argument via `tracing::Span::current().record("target", &target)` AFTER parsing.

11) Word-ban scrub MUST cover the production-side of `server.rs` (NOT the `#[cfg(test)]` carve-out). Pre-flight grep BEFORE first push: `grep -niE 'mock|fake|stub' crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs | grep -v '#\[cfg(test)\]' | grep -v 'pub(super) fn' | head -20` — production-side hits MUST be empty. Test-side `ScriptedFakeSerenaClient` / `TestG7Source` / `TestG8Source` are exempt under DEC-0008 §4 dependency-inversion seam.

12) Module-coherence per DEC-0005: BUNDLE in ONE feat commit per feature: feat-1 (F10) — `handle_check_quality` body + `with_g7_sources` / `with_g8_sources` builder methods + `McpServer.g7_sources` / `g8_sources` fields + dispatch chain wiring + frozen `test_check_quality_tool` test + lib.rs re-exports for F10's wire types. feat-2 (F15) — `handle_type_check` body + `with_diagnostics_client` builder + `McpServer.diagnostics_client` field + dispatch chain wiring + frozen `test_type_check_tool` test + lib.rs re-exports for F15's wire types. NO splits like `handler-without-test` or `test-without-handler` — they produce stub-shaped intermediate states critic flags.

13) Pre-emptively carve out `ucil-build/verification-reports/**` AND `ucil-build/escalations/**` from `forbidden_paths` (see WO-0067 scope_in #42 standing template). The phase-1/2/3 effectiveness-evaluator + gate-check child agents commit refresh artefacts during gate execution.

14) Standing-protocol coverage workaround per WO-0058..WO-0086 (now 42+ WOs deep): the `coverage-gate.sh` sccache `RUSTC_WRAPPER` interaction reports `cargo llvm-cov errored` for `ucil-core` / `ucil-embeddings` / sometimes `ucil-daemon`. Skip the `[FAIL] coverage gate: ucil-{core,embeddings,daemon}` lines and report the AC23 standing-protocol substantive measurement via `env -u RUSTC_WRAPPER cargo llvm-cov --package ucil-daemon --summary-only --json | jq '.data[0].totals.lines.percent'`. The actual `ucil-daemon` coverage MUST be ≥ 85% per AC23.

15) Lessons-applied (carry forward): (a) DEC-0007 module-root frozen tests (now 7 WOs deep through WO-0086 for daemon-side tests); (b) DEC-0008 §4 dependency-inversion seam for TestG7Source / TestG8Source / ScriptedFakeSerenaClient (now 6 WOs deep); (c) WO-0085 §planner 'drop tracing fields(...) numeric casts'; (d) WO-0086 §planner 'AC commit-cadence vs scope_in cohesion — align AC to scope_in 1-coherent-unit-per-feature' (this WO prescribes 4 commits = 2 feat + 1 RFR + 1 lib.rs/Cargo trim if needed); (e) WO-0085 §executor 'always RFR-disclose `.unwrap()` / `.expect()` shipped in production code'; (f) tracing carve-out for pure-deterministic CPU-bound functions does NOT apply here — both handlers are async/IO so DO carry `#[tracing::instrument]`.
scope_out: 1) Production-wiring of REAL `LspDiagnosticsG7Source` / `EslintG7Source` / `RuffG7Source` / `SemgrepG7Source` / `ConventionTestG8Source` / `ImportTestG8Source` / `KgRelationsTestG8Source` impls into daemon startup orchestration (`lifecycle.rs` boot-time wiring, `McpServer::with_default_g7_g8_sources()` factory). This is the SAME deferral as WO-0083 G4 / WO-0085 G7 / WO-0089 G8 backbone WOs — production-wiring follow-up WO bundles the daemon-startup orchestration registration with the real source impls. F10 / F15 acceptance only requires the dispatch handler + test-source-injected integration test.

2) `review_changes` MCP tool (P3-W11-F11) — depends on F10 being shipped first; subsequent WO emits F11 as a solo-feature WO that wires the diff-analysis / blast-radius / quality + test composition into a single response.

3) Feedback loop post-hoc analyser (P3-W11-F12) — different architectural axis (post-response session-state mutation, NOT MCP tool dispatch). Subsequent WO bundles F12 with response-assembly carry-forwards.
4) Warm processors (P3-W10-F13) — different architectural axis (rule-based scheduled jobs over the warm tier, NOT MCP tool dispatch). Subsequent WO.
5) G5 (Context) parallel query (P3-W10-F04) — different architectural axis (G-source backbone + parallel orchestrator, NOT MCP tool dispatch). Subsequent WO.

6) Integration test suites P3-W9-F11 (incremental), P3-W11-F13 (quality pipeline), P3-W11-F14 (testing pipeline), P3-W11-F16 (full query pipeline) — different architectural axis (additive `tests/integration/test_*.rs` files using the WO-0079 / WO-0085 / WO-0089 docker fixtures). Subsequent WO bundles 2-4 of these together per the test-suite-bundle template.

7) Modifying any pre-existing `handle_*` body in `server.rs` (`handle_find_definition`, `handle_get_conventions`, `handle_search_code`, etc.). This WO is purely ADDITIVE — only NEW handler functions and dispatch-chain ELSE-IF branch additions.

8) Adding new workspace-level Cargo deps. The required types (`Diagnostic`, `DiagnosticSeverity`, `Language`, `DiagnosticsClient`) are ALREADY exported from `ucil-lsp-diagnostics` (which is already a `ucil-daemon` workspace dep). The G7 / G8 types are ALREADY in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/g7.rs` + `g8.rs`. NO new deps needed.

9) Standing carry-forwards (NOT new debt): coverage-gate.sh sccache `RUSTC_WRAPPER` workaround (now 42+ WOs deep — Bucket B / Bucket D triage candidate); reality-check.sh pre-existing-stash bug (now 16+ WOs deep — verifier MUST treat as informational only per WO-0067 §verifier lesson, M1+M2+M3 in-place mutation contract is the authoritative anti-laziness layer); effectiveness-gate.sh claude-p sub-session timeout flake (3 open escalations); 'no merge commits introduced on feat' via `git log feat ^main --merges = 0`.
10) `attempts >= 10` features blocked at the planner level — F10 + F15 both have `attempts: 0` per the feature-list snapshot at WO-execution time.
acceptance: F10: `cargo test -p ucil-daemon server::test_check_quality_tool` exits 0 from a `cargo clean` baseline.

F10: The frozen test asserts SA1-SA6 covering issue count, severity vocabulary canary (lowercase `critical`), untested-function count + path, master_timed_out=false, and parallelism wall-clock < 5000 ms.
F10: Mutation M1 (drop G7 from parallel join) flips PASS → FAIL with `(SA1) issues[] length; left: 0, right: 3`.
F10: Mutation M2 (drop G8 from parallel join) flips PASS → FAIL with `(SA3) untested_functions[] length; left: 0, right: 2`.
F15: `cargo test -p ucil-daemon server::test_type_check_tool` exits 0 from a `cargo clean` baseline.

F15: The frozen test asserts SA1-SA5 covering type-error count (Warning + non-type-error filtered out), all severities are `error`, all 3 languages covered (rust/python/typescript), files_checked + files_skipped meta fields.
F15: Mutation M3 (drop type-error filter) flips PASS → FAIL with `(SA1) errors[] length; left: 5, right: 3`.
Both: Production-side word-ban scrub (`grep -niE 'mock|fake|stub' crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs | grep -v '#\[cfg(test)\]'`) returns empty.
Both: AC23 standing-protocol substantive coverage on `ucil-daemon` ≥ 85% via `env -u RUSTC_WRAPPER cargo llvm-cov --package ucil-daemon --summary-only --json | jq '.data[0].totals.lines.percent'`.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: cargo test -p ucil-daemon server::test_check_quality_tool exits 0 from a cargo clean baseline
AC2: cargo test -p ucil-daemon server::test_type_check_tool exits 0 from a cargo clean baseline
AC3: cargo build -p ucil-daemon exits 0
AC4: cargo clippy -p ucil-daemon -- -D warnings exits 0
AC5: cargo fmt --check -p ucil-daemon exits 0

AC7: grep -nE '^[[:space:]]*(pub )?(async )?fn test_type_check_tool\b' crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs returns at least 1 line and the matched line is at MODULE ROOT
AC8: grep -nE 'fn handle_check_quality\b' crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs returns at least 1 line
AC9: grep -nE 'fn handle_type_check\b' crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs returns at least 1 line
AC10: grep -E '"check_quality"' crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs returns at least 2 lines (one for the existing ToolDescriptor entry around line 338, one for the new dispatch-chain branch)
AC11: grep -E '"type_check"' crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs returns at least 2 lines (one for ToolDescriptor entry around line 354, one for new dispatch-chain branch)
AC12: grep -nE '#\[tracing::instrument\(name = "ucil\.tool\.check_quality"\)\]' crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs returns at least 1 line
AC13: grep -nE '#\[tracing::instrument\(name = "ucil\.tool\.type_check"\)\]' crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs returns at least 1 line
AC14: grep -E 'tokio::join!|tokio::try_join!' crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs | grep -E 'execute_g7|execute_g8' returns at least 1 line (parallel fan-out evidence)
AC15: grep -nE 'fn with_g7_sources|fn with_g8_sources|fn with_diagnostics_client' crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs returns at least 3 lines (builder methods)

AC16: Production-side word-ban: grep -niE 'mock|fake|stub' crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs | awk -F: '$2+0 < FIRST_TEST_LINE' (where FIRST_TEST_LINE is the first '#[cfg(test)]' line) returns empty — production code carries NO mock/fake/stub identifiers

AC17: M1 mutation (drop execute_g7 — replace `let (g7_outcome, g8_outcome) = tokio::join!(...)` with `let g8_outcome = execute_g8(...).await; let g7_outcome = G7Outcome::default();`) makes test_check_quality_tool FAIL with `(SA1) issues[] length` panic body; restore via `git checkout -- crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs` + md5sum verify
AC18: M2 mutation (drop execute_g8 — symmetric to M1 on the G8 side) makes test_check_quality_tool FAIL with `(SA3) untested_functions[] length` panic body; restore + md5sum verify

AC19: M3 mutation (drop type-error filter — `.filter(|d| d.severity == DiagnosticSeverity::Error && IS_TYPE_ERROR_SOURCE(...))` → `.filter(|_| true)`) makes test_type_check_tool FAIL with `(SA1) errors[] length` panic body; restore + md5sum verify
AC20: AC23-style standing-protocol substantive coverage: env -u RUSTC_WRAPPER cargo llvm-cov --package ucil-daemon --summary-only --json | jq '.data[0].totals.lines.percent' >= 85.0

AC21: scripts/gate-check.sh 3 — INFORMATIONAL only (the standing coverage-gate sccache workaround means gate-check may exit 1 on coverage sub-checks; per scope_out #9 standing-protocol carry-forward, the verifier captures this in the verification report deviation section); the standing-protocol AC20 substantive measure is the binding floor
AC22: scripts/gate/phase-3.sh — INFORMATIONAL only (same standing-protocol carry-forward as AC21)

AC24: Branch commits at most 6 (1 baseline + 2 feat + 1 RFR + at most 1 lib.rs/Cargo trim if needed + at most 1 doc-rot scrub) per scope_in #12 module-coherence + WO-0086 §planner AC commit-cadence vs scope_in cohesion alignment

AC25: ucil-build/work-orders/0090-ready-for-review.md ships with: (a) per-AC verification table, (b) M1/M2/M3 mutation contract pre-baked with patch + restore command + targeted SA + md5sum snapshot reference path, (c) explicit `## Disclosed deviations` section enumerating any standing carry-forwards (coverage-gate sccache, reality-check stash, AC21/AC22), (d) production-code `.unwrap()` / `.expect()` enumeration with `# Panics` rustdoc justification per WO-0085 §executor lesson, (e) commit-cadence vs scope_in #12 alignment statement
